title: Associate Executive / Senior Executive Assistant, Inpatient Ops (NUHS@Home)(9240)
description:
Join our team and help transform the healthcare landscape in Singapore! We - the NUHS@Home team - are a dynamic new home hospital service looking for passionate and innovative administrative staff to co-create with us.

The job holder will support the day-to-day operational and administrative tasks for NUHS@Home.

We're looking for people with new ideas and a can-do attitude. People who are willing to think outside the box and embrace innovation. People who never give up and are always striving to improve. If you're a proactive problem-solver who is passionate about healthcare and wants to be part of an exciting new home hospital service, we want to hear from you.

Job Responsibilities

1. Procurement

  • Manage the end-to-end procurement process, including sourcing, purchasing, and vendor management.
  • Coordinate with vendors on delivery arrangements and ensure timely submission of invoices.
  • Monitor and track utilization of all supplies to maintain optimal inventory levels.

2. Asset Management

  • Maintain and update asset records regularly to ensure accuracy.
  • Provide first-level troubleshooting for IT-related issues and escalate to relevant parties when necessary.

3. Staff Operations Support

  • Facilitate onboarding and offboarding processes for NUHS@Home staff.
  • Assist in manpower planning, roster scheduling, and verification of monthly timesheets.

4. Stakeholder Engagement

  • Liaise with internal and external stakeholders to ensure timely and accurate processing of monthly invoices.

5. General Office Support

  • Manage office supplies inventory (e.g., stationery, printing materials).
  • Ensure accuracy in data entry and record-keeping.
  • Process monthly and ad-hoc claims for the team.

6. Other Ad-hoc Duties

  • Undertake additional administrative tasks as required to support operational needs.

Job Requirements

  • Minimum Diploma
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (e.g. Teams, Excel, Outlook)
  • Strong communication skills with attention to details
  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment
  • Highly organised with positive work attitude
  • Prior experience or knowledge in a hospital setting is advantageous but not mandatory

postedCompany.description:
The National University Health System (NUHS) plays a key, contributing role in Singapore’s healthcare. Our versatile and experienced team of healthcare professionals has only one aim - to help people get better, and live healthier and longer lives.

We do this by improving medical care, lowering disease recurrence, producing better healthcare professionals, and turning research findings into better medical treatments for diseases that affect Singaporeans. And the reason we’re able to do these things is because we have the best and most dedicated people. Come join us and help shape medicine for our future.
postedCompany.name: NATIONAL UNIVERSITY HEALTH SYSTEM PTE. LTD.

districts.location: D05 Clementi New Town, Hong Leong Garden, Pasir Panjang
